design_philosophy.core_loop: Select and control individual footballer, execute passing/shooting/movement mechanics, compete against AI or human opponents across match duration
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time tactical decision-making between ball possession and defensive positioning; managing player stamina and positioning across dynamic play
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control a professional footballer within a realistic league simulation; manage team tactics and execute plays with authentic soccer mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasis on realistic soccer simulation through removal of one-touch passing (requiring deliberate ball control); isometric perspective choice provides strategic overhead view while maintaining tactical intimacy; deliberate gameplay refinement of weak points (passing, set-pieces, goalkeeping) rather than wholesale redesign

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: AI opponent difficulty likely adjustable; four-player simultaneous multiplayer allows competitive balancing through team selection and player skill

progression.unlock_mechanism: Club and team selection from six European leagues plus Brazilian and American teams; penalty shootout resolution for drawn matches
replayability_hooks: Multiple club sides across different leagues
Four-player simultaneous multiplayer with mixed team/opponent control
Match resolution through penalty shootouts adds variable outcomes
Tactical variety through different team selections

world_building_techniques: Authentic league representation (Premier League, Ligue 1, Bundesliga, Serie A, Eredivisie, La Liga)
Club licensing (with fictional player names due to licensing constraints)
Geographic soccer landscape through team/league selection

environmental_storytelling: Authentic stadium representation through licensed clubs
Isometric perspective reveals field positioning and tactical spacing
Set-piece mechanics (penalties, free kicks) create recognizable soccer scenarios
